result:
All three operations are implemented and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

What I added to `CsvRow.cs`:

- **`EscapeField`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list naturally yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** — a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal `"`, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. Each comma consumed continues the loop, so a trailing comma produces a final empty field, and the empty line parses to `[""]`.

Edge cases line up with the spec: `JoinRow([])` is `""`, which `SplitRow` parses as one empty field (the documented exception), and `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trips exactly for any non-empty list since every field needing quoting is quoted on the way out and unquoted symmetrically on the way back. `NeedsQuoting` is untouched. Build succeeded on the first cycle with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
